author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Wide-ranging session covering repo housekeeping, M365 agent instructions drafting, and deep coverage math analysis. Started by committing and pushing pending changes, then worked on improving Copilot agent instructions to 8000-character limit and updating them to handle both personal project assistance and team questions. Explored repo organization suggestions. Later shifted to a detailed audit of pipeline logic issues flagged by a Copilot review, specifically around the coverage percentage denominator, pre-scored sections leaking into scoring, and whether an integration fixture for the AI step was truly missing. Session hit rate limits toward the end while investigating where `render_from_results.py` pulls its denominator from `scoring.py`.
problems: M365 declarative agent instructions were insufficient â€” needed expansion to 8000-character limit covering both personal use and team Q&A
Repo organization was messy; .md files could not be attached to agents directly (needed Word conversions)
Coverage math may be inflated: pre-scored sections could leak into denominator (Defect 3)
test count in PROJECT_BRIEFING.md was stale (claimed 210, actual run showed 233)
No single pytest integration fixture covering the full Phase1â†’verify_groundingâ†’Phase2 seam
approaches: Committed and pushed all pending changes to clean up working tree
Drafted expanded M365 agent instructions combining project assistant and team Q&A roles
Created Word versions of key .md files for attachment to agents
Reviewed PROJECT_BRIEFING.md claims against actual test run output
Investigated whether Defect 3 (pre-scored sections denominator inflation) was already documented as a known gap
Identified entry point for denominator computation: render_from_results.py imports from scripts/scoring.py
results: Agent instructions expanded and updated for both personal and team use
Confirmed Defect 3 is already listed as a known gap in PROJECT_BRIEFING
Confirmed stale test count (210 vs 233) as immediate fix target
Denominator logic traced to scripts/scoring.py via render_from_results.py

decisions.decision: A dedicated Agent B Project Assistant folder should be created to hold all files the agent needs, updated each time the project updates
Integration fixture covering Phase1â†’LLMâ†’Phase2 seam is needed as a pytest test using a static known-good analysis.json

open_questions: What is the exact filter logic in scoring.py that excludes pre-scored sections from the denominator?
Is the stale test count in PROJECT_BRIEFING.md the only documentation drift?
